title: ConvSim Safety Policy
description: Validates a safety policy file that governs content boundaries for a scenario. Categories listed here are enforced by the input router before the NPC runtime is called (SPEC §13.2).

content_categories.description: Explicit allow/block rules per content category. Global non-overridable categories (minors_romantic_or_sexual, self_harm_crisis) are enforced by the input router regardless of what is declared here.

minors_romantic_or_sexual.description: Romantic or sexual content involving minors. Global non-overridable — always stop.

real_person_impersonation.description: Impersonation of an identifiable real person. 'block' is a legacy value that maps to 'stop'; prefer 'stop' in new policies.

voice_cloning_request.description: Requests to clone, copy, or deepfake a voice.

medical_or_therapy_claim.description: Medical diagnosis, prescription, or therapy-as-treatment claims.

legal_claim.description: Professional legal advice or opinion claims.

criminal_instruction.description: Instructions for criminal activity or physical harm.

harassment_extreme.description: Extreme harassment, credible threats, or targeted abuse.

self_harm_crisis.description: Self-harm or suicidal ideation. Global non-overridable — always stop and surface real crisis resources.

nsfw_sexual.description: Legacy alias for nsfw_sexual_content. Prefer nsfw_sexual_content in new policies.

violence_graphic.description: Graphic violence. Enforced by the NPC output validator; not currently checked by the input router.

instructional_criminal.description: Legacy alias for criminal_instruction. Prefer criminal_instruction in new policies.

medical_professional_advice.description: Legacy alias for medical_or_therapy_claim. Prefer medical_or_therapy_claim in new policies.

crisis_content.description: Legacy alias for self_harm_crisis. Prefer self_harm_crisis in new policies.

redirect_message.description: Fallback redirect message displayed to the player when the category has no per-category message configured.

allow_profanity.description: Whether mild profanity is allowed in the NPC's dialogue.

content_rating_cap.description: Maximum content rating this policy permits.
